Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Deshawn Hartley and the duplicate flight situation using search_emails with queries like "Deshawn Hartley" or "flight confirmation" or "Tampa Denver." The agent needs to find the full correspondence chain: Suki Patel's booking request to Michael Stern at Atlas Corporate Travel on April 7 requesting a morning Frontier flight on April 13 (email_mosaic_hartley_suki_atlas_booking), Atlas's confirmation of Frontier F9 1847 departing 7:15 AM at $312 corporate rate with confirmation FRTPA8247 (email_mosaic_hartley_atlas_suki_confirm), Suki's forwarded confirmation to Deshawn (email_mosaic_hartley_suki_confirm_fwd), Blessing Okafor's separate booking request to Atlas on April 9 for an afternoon economy flight on the same date (email_mosaic_hartley_blessing_atlas_booking), Atlas's confirmation of Spirit NK 324 departing 2:30 PM at $487 standard fare with confirmation NKTPA5091 and a note that no corporate rate code was provided (email_mosaic_hartley_atlas_blessing_confirm), Blessing's forwarded confirmation to Deshawn (email_mosaic_hartley_blessing_confirm_fwd), Deshawn's email to support@moveops.com on April 10 asking which flight is his (email_mosaic_hartley_support_dupe, marked unread), Blessing's cancellation request to Atlas on April 10 (email_mosaic_hartley_blessing_atlas_cancel), and Atlas's reply on April 11 stating the Spirit ticket is non-refundable because it was issued under Spirit's instant-issue policy and the 24-hour federal cancellation window had passed, with three options offered: a $487 travel credit valid 90 days, a name change for $75, or forfeiture at departure (email_mosaic_hartley_atlas_blessing_norefund).
Search emails for messages from Deshawn Hartley to other MoveOps employees using search_emails with queries like "Deshawn" or "deshawn.hartley" or "Denver flight." The agent needs to discover that Deshawn also emailed emeka.diallo@moveops.com on April 10 (email_mosaic_hartley_emeka_dupe) asking about the two flight confirmations. That email was read but never answered, confirming a second communication gap beyond the unanswered support ticket.
Search Slack for internal discussion of the duplicate booking using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Deshawn" or "duplicate" or "Spirit" or "flight" filtered to the #operations channel (C006). The agent needs to find the thread started by Suki Patel on April 10 flagging that Atlas received a second booking for Deshawn that she did not make. Blessing replied acknowledging she pulled the Mosaic flight tasks from the April capacity list without checking whether anyone had already handled them. Chloe Vance replied calling it the second duplicate vendor booking this quarter and telling Blessing to confirm the cancellation went through and check whether Atlas already charged them. Blessing posted a follow-up on April 11 confirming that Atlas said the Spirit ticket is non-refundable, with a $487 travel credit valid 90 days or a name change for $75, and that she had not accepted either option.
Search Airtable for Deshawn Hartley's relocation record using airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value "Deshawn Hartley." The agent should find record recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver showing Company: Mosaic Robotics, Origin: Tampa FL, Destination: Denver CO, Status: In Progress, Move Start Date: 2026-04-14, Move End Date: 2026-04-19, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel, with special requirements noting a hard deadline for the Automate 2026 trade show (April 21 to 24 at the Denver Convention Center) and booth setup starting April 20. This helps verify whether the move completed on time.
Search Linear for existing tickets related to the duplicate booking using linear_list_issues with queries like "Deshawn" or "duplicate" or "flight" or "Mosaic." The agent needs to find linear_issue_dupe_flight_hartley (number 425, team_operations, assigned to Blessing Okafor, priority P2, due 2026-04-17). The ticket documents both bookings with full details, the failed cancellation attempt, Atlas's non-refundable response, and the fact that Deshawn's support email has not been answered as of the ticket's last update.
Search QuickBooks for the duplicate flight vendor charge using quickbooks_search_bills with queries like "Atlas" or "Spirit" or "Deshawn" or doc number "ACT-2026-0419." The agent needs to find bill_atlas_hartley_dupe for $487.00 dated April 9 with a balance of $487.00, referencing Spirit NK 324 TPA to DEN for Deshawn Hartley and noting it is a duplicate of Frontier F9 1847 on the same date. This confirms MoveOps was billed for the duplicate flight and the charge is still outstanding.
Search CRM for the Mosaic Robotics company record and related deals using crm_search_companies with name "Mosaic" and crm_search_deals or crm_list_engagements. The agent should find company_mosaic along with active deals including deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 for Deshawn's relocation ($1,520), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk ($2,700, at-risk due to prototype damage), and deal_mosaic_q2 ($4,500 Q2 renewal). The agent should also pull engagement notes documenting the April 2026 relocation wave and the Zara Okoye prototype damage situation. This builds the full account picture Omar needs so he is not blindsided if Tamara Byrd calls in.
Search emails and Slack for broader Mosaic Robotics context using search_emails and conversations_search_messages with queries like "Mosaic" or "Zara Okoye" or "prototype" or "Andre Washington" or "expense" or "ExpenseBot." The agent needs to discover: the Zara Okoye prototype damage incident where a $90,000 robotic arm was damaged during the Savannah to Atlanta relocation with a $40,000 insurance coverage gap for MoveOps, the suspicious receipt flag on Andre Washington's $89 ergonomic keyboard submission (STIP-2026-0094, duplicate hash matching Kevin Tran at Vectral), and the ExpenseBot false approvals for Mosaic employees where the auto-categorization tool had a malformed excluded_categories field causing coworking space expenses to be wrongly approved. These are the fragments Omar mentioned hearing about.
Look up contact email addresses for Deshawn Hartley, Julian Brooks, and Emeka Diallo using contacts_search_contacts with queries "Deshawn Hartley," "Julian Brooks," and "Emeka Diallo." The agent needs to confirm deshawn.hartley@mosaicrobotics.com, julian.brooks@moveops.com, and emeka.diallo@moveops.com before sending outbound communications.
Look up the #customer-support Slack channel using channels_list to retrieve the channel ID (C007) needed for posting the team summary.
Reply to Deshawn Hartley's support email using reply_to_email with the parent email ID email_mosaic_hartley_support_dupe. The reply should come from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com, apologize for the delay in responding, confirm that his correct flight was the Frontier F9 1847 at 7:15 AM on April 13 with confirmation FRTPA8247, explain that the second Spirit confirmation was a duplicate booking that has been identified and is being resolved internally, and keep the tone factual without going into internal blame.
Add a comment to the existing Linear tracking ticket for the duplicate booking (linear_issue_dupe_flight_hartley, issue 425) using linear_create_comment. The comment should note that Deshawn's April 10 support email went unanswered for over two weeks, that Omar has now replied to the client confirming the Frontier booking as the correct one, and that the $487 non-refundable charge from Atlas and the travel credit decision remain unresolved.
Update Deshawn Hartley's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records on record recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver (base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblRelocations01). Add a note about the duplicate flight booking, the confirmed correct booking (Frontier F9 1847, FRTPA8247), and the outstanding $487 vendor charge from Atlas for the duplicate Spirit ticket.
Create an engagement note on the Mosaic Robotics CRM account using crm_create_engagement with company_ids referencing company_mosaic. The engagement should document the duplicate flight booking incident, the two-week support response gap, the $487 outstanding vendor charge from Atlas, and the current resolution status so the account team has visibility before any interaction with Tamara Byrd.
Send an email to Julian Brooks using send_email from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to julian.brooks@moveops.com. The email should lay out the full breakdown: the timeline of the duplicate booking (Suki booked Frontier on April 7 at $312 corporate rate, Blessing booked Spirit on April 9 at $487 standard fare after pulling from the capacity list without checking), the failed cancellation and non-refundable outcome from Atlas, the $487 outstanding vendor charge, the fact that Deshawn's support email sat unanswered for over two weeks, that Deshawn also emailed Emeka directly with no response, and Chloe's note in #operations that this is the second duplicate vendor booking this quarter.
Send a heads-up email to Emeka Diallo using send_email from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com. The email should let Emeka know that Deshawn Hartley reached out to both the support queue and Emeka directly about the duplicate flight confirmations, that Omar has now replied to Deshawn and confirmed the correct booking, and that there is a $487 outstanding vendor charge from Atlas for the duplicate that the account team should be aware of before Emeka's next check-in with Tamara Byrd at Mosaic.
Post a summary in the #customer-support Slack channel (C007) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover that Deshawn Hartley from Mosaic Robotics emailed support on April 10 about two conflicting flight confirmations for his Tampa to Denver relocation, the ticket went unanswered for over two weeks, Omar has now responded and confirmed the correct Frontier booking, a duplicate Spirit flight was booked by a coordinator who did not check existing bookings, the cancellation failed and there is a $487 non-refundable charge from Atlas still unresolved, and Julian has been briefed on the full situation.
Check Omar's and Julian's calendars for availability early in the week of April 28 using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to or calendar_search_events. Then schedule a thirty-minute process review meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event with Omar Ibrahim and Julian Brooks as attendees, covering the duplicate booking process gap and the two-week support response gap to discuss how to catch these before they reach the client.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
